Small Batch Roaster
Curious Coffee · Ann Arbor, MI

Coffee that makes
you ask questions.

Single-origin. Farm-direct. Roasted in obsessively small batches.

Explore the Roast Shop Now
The Story

High quality coffee. Coffee education.

01

One cherry, two beans.

Curious Coffee has two main pillars: high quality coffee and coffee education. Two pursuits, one passion. Everything we do connects back to these roots.

02

The coffee is the heart of it.

We source exceptional green coffees and roast them light to make them shine. No dark roasting to hide flaws. Just great coffee, treated with care and respect.

03

For curious coffee minds.

We run coffee courses to suit every level. Whether you are just starting out or dialling in your espresso technique, we want to share everything we know.

Roasting Approach

Light roasts.
Big flavours.

The coffee is the heart of it. I aim to source exceptional green coffees and roast them light to make them shine.

Light roasting preserves the unique character that each farmer has worked so hard to develop. It keeps the brightness, the complexity, the story of where the coffee came from.

Every roast is small enough that we can control every variable. No shortcuts. If it is not exceptional, it does not make the cut.

Hover to meet Mike

Why We Are Different

This is what curiosity tastes like.

Obsessive Detail

We cup every roast. If it is not exceptional, it does not make the cut.

Farm Direct

We source from specific farms, specific people. Every bag tells a real story.

Flavor First

We chase unusual, delicious, and surprising. Not safe. Curious.

Currently in the Roastery

What is in the bag right now.

Our lineup rotates as we find exceptional lots. Hover any card to learn more about the coffee and the farm.

Colombia · Natural

Jairo Arcila

Strawberry Castillo

Premium
🍓 StrawberryCantaloupe🌸 Floral
$27.50 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Quindio · 1450-1500m · Castillo

Jairo Arcila · Strawberry Castillo

Sunshine in a cup! Grown on the Santa Monica farm. 72-hour dry anaerobic fermentation with strawberries and wine yeast. Notes of strawberry, cantaloupe, lime, brown sugar, pineapple, guava and rose hip. Brilliant as filter or aeropress.

Buy Now $27.50
Colombia · Honey Process

Jairo Arcila

Peach Pink Bourbon

Premium
🍑 PeachBergamotEarl Grey
$28.00 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Quindio · 1450-1500m · Castillo

Jairo Arcila · Peach Pink Bourbon

Peachy goodness just in time for spring! Reminiscent of biting into a juicy peach. 72-hour dry anaerobic fermentation with fresh peaches and wine yeast. Notes of peach, bergamot, brown sugar, lime and earl grey. Possibly the best co-ferment yet.

Buy Now $28.00
Colombia · Anaerobic Natural

Edwin Noreña

Geisha Intenso

Premium
🌸 Jasmine🍍 Tropical🍵 Tea-like
$32.50 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Anaerobic Natural · Geisha

Edwin Noreña · Geisha Intenso

An exceptional Colombian geisha with intensive anaerobic natural processing. Delicate jasmine florals, tropical fruit complexity and tea-like clarity. Roasted primarily for filter but outstanding as espresso too.

Colombia · Anaerobic Natural

Dinastía

Geisha · Special Release

Premium
🍓 StrawberryWhite Chocolate🍍 Tropical
$38.50 Buy This Bag
Huila, Colombia · Farm: La Dinastía · Wilder Lazo

Dinastía · Geisha · Special Release

What a stunner! From producer Wilder Lazo at La Dinastía. Hot: strawberry and white chocolate prevail. As it cools: passion fruit and tropical florals emerge. Not funky, so it pleases washed and natural lovers alike. Rest 10 days minimum.

Buy Now $38.50
Colombia · Anaerobic Washed

Bella Alejandria

Geisha · Special Release

Premium
🌺 FloralSweet OrangeChamomile
$38.50 Buy This Bag
Huila, Colombia · Farm: Bella Alejandria · Wilder Lazo

Bella Alejandria · Geisha · Special Release

Intense florality, incredible sweetness and a long clean finish. Pronounced rose, jasmine, chamomile and hibiscus. Restrained acidity accentuates the sweetness. 36-hour anaerobic washed fermentation. Exceptional as filter and espresso. Rest 14 days minimum.

Buy Now $38.50
Colombia · Washed

Paola Trujillo

Typica

Premium
Stone FruitMuscovadoLime
$30.00 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Caldono, Cauca · Farm: Patio Bonito

Paola Trujillo · Typica

A beautiful washed typica with mouthwatering stone fruit, sweet muscovado sugar and zingy lime. Everything you want from a washed process gem. Bright, juicy and clean. Rest 2 weeks minimum.

Honduras · Low Intervention Washed

El Puente

SL-28

Premium
Yellow PlumJasminePomelo
$25.00 Buy This Bag
Honduras · Chinacla, La Paz · 1580m · SL-28

El Puente · SL-28

A captivating low intervention washed coffee with real depth for fans of terroir-driven cups. Notes of yellow plum, jasmine, pomelo, herbaceous, rhubarb and ginger. Bright acidity, medium sweetness. Likes a solid 3 weeks rest to really open up.

Buy Now $25.00
Colombia · Honey Process

Jairo Arcila

Banana Co-Ferment

Premium
🍌 BananaCaramelLime
$26.50 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Quindio · 1500m · Santa Monica Farm

Jairo Arcila · Banana Co-Ferment

This coffee is bananas! As espresso with milk it tastes like bananas Foster. Black it tastes like fresh bananas. Banana co-ferment honey process. Notes of banana, caramel and lime. Great as the base for a milk drink. Rest just 3-7 days.

Buy Now $26.50
Colombia · Washed

Jairo Arcila

Washed Geisha

Premium
🌺 RoseMandarinApple
$30.00 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Quindio · 1500m · Santa Monica Farm

Jairo Arcila · Washed Geisha

A wonderful delicate washed geisha with notes of rose, mandarin, apple, lemongrass, lime and black tea. 30-hour underwater fermentation. Filter is the first choice but brave espresso lovers will be very well rewarded. Rest 10-14 days minimum.

Buy Now $30.00
Colombia · Semi-Washed

Andrés Martinez

Geisha

Premium
🍑 PeachRoseVanilla
$25.00 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Cauca · 1750m · Farm: El Sendero

Andrés Martinez · Geisha

Sweet, floral and absolutely delicious! 48-hour fermentation with beer yeast then 24 hours in mucilage stirred every 3-4 hours. Two-stage drying for exceptional sweetness and silkiness. Notes of peach, rose, vanilla, lavender and apricot. Filter or cold brew recommended.

Buy Now $25.00
Colombia · Semi-Washed

Andrés Martinez

Caturra Floral

Standard
Earl GreyOrange BlossomHoney
$26.00 Buy This Bag
Colombia · Cauca · 1750m · Farm: El Sendero

Andrés Martinez · Caturra Floral

Delicate florality, exceptional balance with a creamy mouthfeel and honey-like sweetness. 48-hour cherry fermentation then depulped and fermented again for 24 hours. Notes of earl grey, orange blossom, caramel, mandarin, honey and jasmine.

Buy Now $26.00
Brazil · Double Fermentation

Fazenda Guariroba

Pink Bourbon

Standard
🌼 FloralsSweet GingerChocolate
$27.50 Buy This Bag
Minas Gerais, Brazil · 1200m · Pink Bourbon

Fazenda Guariroba · Pink Bourbon

A very different Brazilian coffee! 36-hour bioreactor fermentation then pulped and fermented again for 36 more hours. Medium acidity, high sweetness, beautifully clean. Florals, sweet ginger, pepper and chocolate. Roasted light for filter. 10oz bags.

Buy Now $27.50

Milk ChocolateNutsGreen Apple

Peru Organic · Washed · Fair Trade

A real crowd pleaser full of milk chocolate and nutty goodness! From 124 women producers of APROCCURMA. Notes of milk chocolate, nuts, green apple and lime. Works beautifully as espresso or drip. Fair Trade and Organic certified. 10oz bags.

Colombia · Honey Process

Sebastian Ramirez

Strawberry Caturra

Premium
🍓 StrawberryCherryChocolate
$27.50 Out of Stock
Colombia · Quindio, Armenia · 1750m · Farm: El Placer

Sebastian Ramirez · Strawberry Caturra

This strawberry co-ferment tastes like strawberry cake in a cup! Bold and jammy (unlike the more delicate Jairo Arcila version). 120-hour anaerobic fermentation with wine yeast then 72 hours with CO2 injection and strawberries added. Notes of strawberry, cherry and chocolate. More coming in April.

Out of Stock
Out of Stock
Colombia · White Honey

Sebastian Ramirez

White Honey Geisha

Premium
LavenderOrangeHoney
$36.00 Out of Stock
Colombia · Quindio, Armenia · Farm: El Placer · Geisha

Sebastian Ramirez · White Honey Geisha

Bursting with intense floral notes and the sweetness of honey. Some of the clearest lavender notes we have tasted in a long while. White honey process with 48-hour anaerobic fermentation. A very clean and sweet cup with wonderful intense florals and a long sweet finish.

Blackberry Co-Ferment

Premium
BlackberryDark FruitChocolate
N/A Out of Stock
Colombia · Quindio · Farm: El Placer · Blackberry Co-Ferment

Sebastian Ramirez · Blackberry Co-Ferment

A bold and jammy blackberry co-ferment from Sebastian Ramirez at El Placer farm. Intense dark fruit, blackberry and chocolate character. From a fourth-generation coffee farmer specialising in carbonic maceration and varietal innovation.

Colombia · Natural Thermal Shock

Yessica & Diego Parra

Thermal Shock Sidra

Premium
🍓 StrawberryPassion FruitGummy Bears
$29.50 Out of Stock
Colombia · Huila, Pitalito · 1750m · Farm: El Mirador

Yessica & Diego Parra · Thermal Shock Sidra

Bold and tropical! An innovative thermal shock fermentation: alternating hot and cold water to stress the beans in a controlled way, intensifying sweetness. Notes of strawberry, pink grapefruit, gummy bears, passion fruit, papaya and bright lime. Intense, funky and packed with fruity goodness.

Out of Stock
Out of Stock
Ethiopia · Red Honey Process

Danche

Red Honey

Standard
Sweet OrangeApricot🌸 Florals
$28.50 Out of Stock
Worka, Chelchele, Ethiopia · 1944m · Tracon Coffee · 705 farmers

Danche · Red Honey

Lies between a bright washed Ethiopian and a fruity natural. Very unusual to have this processing method with Ethiopian coffees and the results are excellent. Delicate, sweet and very versatile. Notes of sweet orange, apricot, florals and caramel. Works beautifully on filter, drip and espresso.

Out of Stock
Brazil · Natural

Fazenda Matão

Mundo Novo

Standard
🍫 ChocolateNutsBrown Sugar
$18.50 Buy This Bag
Brazil · Mogiana, São Paulo · 900m · Mundo Novo

Fazenda Matão · Mundo Novo

Chocolaty, nutty, velvety. A real gem of a classic sweet Brazilian natural process coffee. Wonderful as an easy-going espresso, very drinkable as drip and great black or with milk. Notes of chocolate, nuts, velvety brown sugar, clean and very sweet. 10oz bags.

Fazenda São Domingos

Acaía

Standard
Almond🍫 ChocolateBrown Sugar
$18.00 Buy This Bag
Brazil · Mococa, São Paulo · 900m · Acaía · SCA 84.5

Fazenda São Domingos · Acaía

Chocolate and nuts with a silky mouthfeel. The go-to choice for an easy-going espresso, a very drinkable drip coffee and great as a milk drink base. Notes of almond, chocolate, silky brown sugar, clean. SCA score 84.5. 6oz bags.

Guatemala · Washed

Finca El Socorro

Low Caffeine · Laurina

Standard
Toasted AlmondNougatRed Apple
$28.50 Buy This Bag
Guatemala · Palencia · Laurina variety · Cup of Excellence 8x winner

Finca El Socorro · Low Caffeine · Laurina

A sweet, smooth and delicate coffee with only 1/3 to 1/2 the caffeine of other arabica cultivars. High sweetness, low acidity, complex and subtle. Notes of toasted almonds, nougat and red apple. Works beautifully on espresso, milk drinks and pourover. Juan Diego De La Cerda, Cup of Excellence winner 8 times. 10oz bags.

Buy Now $28.50

Luz Helena Salazar · Geisha

Another wonderful coffee from Luz Helena Salazar, wife of Jairo Arcila. Bright, sweet and complex with it all. Washed geisha from Farm La Leona. Notes of florals, blueberry, lime and honey. SCA score 88.75. Roasted for filter but excellent as espresso too. 6oz bags.

Paola Trujillo · Sydra

Wonderful clear lavender notes and great sweetness from Paola Trujillo at Patio Bonito farm. 30-hour underwater fermentation. Sidra is a Bourbon and Typica hybrid originating in Ecuador. Notes of lavender, red fruits, brown sugar, cacao nibs and lime. Excellent as filter, can also shine as espresso. 10oz bags.

Lemon CakeChaiLavender

Minas Gerais, Brazil · 1200m · Topázio · Cup of Excellence farm

Fazenda Guariroba · Topázio

Another amazing coffee from Fazenda Guariroba. Challenges preconceptions of what Brazilian coffee tastes like. Double fermentation process. Notes of lemon cake, chai spice, lavender and almonds. Sweet, complex and silky. Roasted light for filter. 6oz bags.

In-Person · Ann Arbor Only

The Curious Coffee Club

So many of you have asked for this and we are delighted to offer it! The Curious Coffee Club is open to all those who are able to make it to Ann Arbor.

☕

Two bags a month

Pick up two bags of your choosing from the available stock on the shelves at the cafe each month.

🤝

Monthly meetups

Mid-month meetups to chat with other coffee enthusiasts, taste something new and ask Mike about the coffees you picked up.

🎓

Workshop discounts

10% off workshops and one-to-one sessions. 5% off all further bags bought at the cafe. Plus special events and sneak peeks at upcoming coffees.

$60 /month
Join the Club

Cafe pick-up only · Select "Cafe Pick Up" at checkout · Cancel anytime

★★★★★

"Earns every star for top quality service and coffee experience. The team and owner clearly know their bean selection, roast, and every method you need to get a surprising mouthful of flavor. Pick your new favourite, and prepare to create reasons for a caffeinated experience provided by someone focused on bringing new coffees to your local palate."

K

Kevin M.

Google Review

★★★★★

"Tried a new place after seeing the sign on a walk and I was rewarded with a great surprise! What a true hidden gem! The baristas were very nice and explained the menu well. I got the iced honey latte and I give it a 9.5/10. Also the atmosphere inside is a very cute vibe. Skip the chains, try Curious!"

S

Sarah L.

Google Review

★★★★★

"Mike and his team are elevating the coffee game in Ann Arbor to unprecedented levels. Mark my words: Curious Coffee is going to be THE coffee spot in Ann Arbor. Curious Coffee is seriously leveling up the Ann Arbor coffee scene."

A

Allison R.

Yelp Review

★★★★★

"My coffee snob friend told me about Curious Coffee and how legit the pour overs were. I am very much not the discerning coffee sort, but I loved my stop in Curious Coffee! I got a honey latte to go with oat milk. Basic order maybe, but this was DELICIOUS!"

J

Jamie T.

Yelp Review

★★★★★

"Mike is a real stickler about process. When you pay for a cup of coffee here, you are in part paying for the endless hours of research that Mike has done to determine the best roast and water chemistry for that coffee bean. Ann Arbor has a number of incredible coffee shops, but Mike has created his niche by really diving deep into the process."

L

Lauren B.

Featured in Current Magazine

★★★★★

"Incredible new coffee spot in Ann Arbor. High-end speciality coffee roaster and cafe combining excellent coffee with warm service. The care and knowledge that goes into every cup is immediately obvious. One of the best coffee experiences in the city."
